channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's Chicago speech will be his first since leaving office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: White House proposes drastic cuts to State Department and funding for UN, NATO and other groups
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House’s Office of Management and Budget has proposed gutting the State Department’s budget by almost 50%, closing a number of overseas diplomatic missions, slashing the number of diplomatic staff, and eliminating funding for nearly all international organizations, includi
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Nancy Mace targeted by Pennsylvania man arrested for threatening Trump
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace's office was alerted to a threat on her life made against a man who was recently arrested for threatening President Trump, a source said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog group exposes red state college administrator explaining 'loophole' around anti-DEI law
item.description: A Florida university system administrator is facing heat after undercover video appears to show her discussing ways her school is skirting the state's anti-DEI law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International students file legal challenges over widespread US visa revocations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several international students who have had their visas revoked in recent weeks have filed lawsuits against the Trump administration, arguing the government denied them due process when it suddenly took away their permission to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University has been arrested at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle And Holly Ramer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After loss to Slotkin, Michigan Republican Mike Rogers launches another Senate bid
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Former U.S. Rep. Mike Rogers is launching another Republican bid for the U.S. Senate in Michigan, following a narrow loss to Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin last year in a hotly contested open race. This time, Rogers is vying for the seat being vacated by Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters widely oppose taxpayer-funded gender surgeries, revealing Democrat Party's vulnerability: poll
item.description: Taxpayer-funded transgender treatments remain widely unpopular among American voters, according to a new poll done by American Principles Project.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump considers pausing his auto tariffs as the world economy endures whiplash
item.description: President Donald Trump says he might temporarily exempt the auto industry from tariffs he previously imposed on the sector, to give carmakers time to adjust their supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bill de Blasio on what's next for the Democratic Party
item.description: De Blasio, who has received fierce criticism from President Trump this week, believes the Democrats blew the presidential election on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: State Department wants staff to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias during Biden's term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Christian world commemorates Holy Week leading up to Easter Sunday, the State Department has issued an appeal for its employees to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias, including formal or informal actions due to opposition to vaccines or personal pronoun choice,
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
